previously_reclassified.reason: Same root-cause class as latex defect: cv_artifact_exercise (when added retroactively) ran or would have run with substituted runtime deps; the deliverable's true behavior in user environment was never verified. Compounded: behavioral defect (Deal button stayed disabled after phase==='resolved') was discovered post-delivery.

gates_re_evaluated.rationale: No state/coordinator/build-complete.json. Run terminated after edge-case-testing without writing the v1.2 handoff signal — same pattern that motivated the v1.2 amendment.

Original blackjack run was missing output/final/, audit/flags.jsonl with final-sweep entry, output/verification/report.json. Subsequent re-runs added these incidentally but the run as-delivered failed delivery checklist.

Sections file lacks machine_checkable_assertions[] on inflection_resolutions. The IP2 'six-deck shoe shuffled per session' lock had no machine-checkable form, which is exactly why the per-round shoe drift went undetected.

Sections lack acceptance_assertions[]. The 'see the round outcome and start a new round' acceptance phrase was prose-only, structurally untestable, and the Deal-button-stays-disabled-after-resolve defect shipped past every gate.
No cv_artifact_exercise was performed. The verification report is static inspection only. This is the gate v1.3 introduced; this run pre-dates it.

Even had cv_artifact_exercise been added retroactively under v1.3 substrate, it would have run in jsdom with stubs (the substrate v1.5 strikes). The deliverable was never loaded the way users open it.
No PNV exists. The prompt's named verb 'play blackjack' was never asserted end-to-end against the deliverable in production fidelity.

incidental_observations: The artifact also has the Deal-button-disabled defect (per the conversation context where another Claude found it post-delivery). That is a real artifact defect on top of the verification regime gaps.
The IP2 shoe drift (per-round vs per-session) is also a real artifact deviation from the locked spec.
This run failed on every load-bearing v1.2-onward gate. It is the canonical example of why the architecture has grown the gates it has.

recovery_options.description: Patch the Deal button (Sev 0 fix already documented in the conversation), patch the per-round shoe (per-session shoe construction), then add v1.2-v1.6 gate artifacts (machine_checkable_assertions, acceptance_assertions, PNV, prompt_verb_analysis, build-complete.json, Historian artifacts), run CV artifact-exercise under v1.5 production fidelity. Substantial patching effort given how many gates failed.

Discard prior outputs and re-run 'build me a web app to play blackjack' under v1.6. Cleaner; the v1.6 architecture would catch the Deal button issue (PNV: 'place a bet, deal, play to resolution, deal again, observe new round') and the shoe drift (machine_checkable_assertion on six-deck-shoe-per-session) automatically.

recommendation_rationale: Nine gate failures span structural (build-complete, Historian artifacts) and behavioral (PNV, production fidelity, acceptance assertions). Patching would touch every layer of the build and still leave the underlying architectural mismatches. A clean v1.6 rebuild is cheaper and produces a v1.6-native run with all required artifacts. Also: v1.6 would catch both real artifact defects (Deal button, per-round shoe) on its first verification pass, providing strong validation of the new gates.
